content: The linguistic double meaning of "reflexion" (spelled the same for both physical reflection of light and metacognitive self-reflection in the original Oxford English spelling) can be leveraged to design a new LLM self-reflection mechanism that mimics how physical light reflection bounces off surfaces to carry information back to its source. This mechanism would require an LLM to "bounce" its own outputs off structured linguistic reference prompts, capturing inconsistencies between its initial output and the reflected information from the reference set the same way physical reflection captures surface shape information in returning light.
